key_insight: The paper extends actor-critic reinforcement learning to continuous-time mean field control with deterministic policies, allowing both dynamics and rewards to depend on the joint state-control distribution.
engineering_relevance: It provides a model-free way to train control policies for large coupled systems such as fleets, crowds, and finance models where aggregate interactions matter.
method: Derives a sensitivity formula for parameterized McKean–Vlasov dynamics in a model-free setting
Builds deterministic policy-gradient expressions using advantage-rate and local value/advantage representations on Wasserstein space
Implements a continuous-time DDPG-style algorithm with particle approximations, measure-aware neural networks, and temporal-difference learning
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on stochastic Cucker–Smale consensus control and optimal liquidation with trade crowding, showing efficiency, stability, and robustness.
watch_item: Performance may depend on accurate particle approximations and stable estimation of measure-derivative terms in high-dimensional systems.
paper_title: Actor-Critic Learning for Extended Mean Field Control with Deterministic Policies
